tasks.name: Create Event Details Server Page Component
tasks.description: Implement the main server component at src/app/[locale]/profile/events/[id]/page.tsx that fetches event data, handles user authentication and access control, and renders the complete event details page. This component serves as the entry point for individual event pages with proper user type differentiation within the profile section.
tasks.notes: Follow the established pattern from src/app/[locale]/profile/events/page.tsx for server component structure. Use createServerSupabaseClient() and getTranslations() utilities. Implement proper access control: researchers can view all published events, organizers can only view their own events, admins can view all events. Handle invalid event IDs with 404 responses.

relatedFiles.description: Main server component file to be created

tasks.verificationCriteria: Server component correctly fetches event data by ID, implements proper user access control (organizers only see own events), handles translation loading, implements proper error handling for invalid IDs, and renders without errors when accessing /[locale]/events/[valid-id] routes.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The main event details server page component at src/app/[locale]/profile/events/[id]/page.tsx has been successfully implemented. It properly fetches event data, handles authentication, access control, and implements the subscription functionality correctly. The component now uses the appropriate RPC function for subscription data with proper TypeScript type handling.

tasks.name: Implement Event Details Layout Container
tasks.description: Create a reusable layout container component that provides consistent structure for the event details page, including breadcrumb navigation, responsive grid layout, and proper spacing. This component wraps all event detail sections and ensures consistent presentation across different user types.
tasks.notes: Reference existing layout components in profile and dashboard pages for consistent styling. Ensure RTL support is properly implemented for Arabic locale. Use Flowbite React components where appropriate. Keep layout clean and focused on content viewing.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Layout component provides proper responsive structure, breadcrumb navigation works correctly, RTL layout displays properly in Arabic locale, component integrates seamlessly with the main page component, and layout is consistent across different user types.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Event Details Layout Container has been successfully implemented as EventDetailsLayout.tsx. The component provides a consistent structure with breadcrumb navigation, a responsive grid layout (1-column on mobile, 4-column grid on desktop), and proper spacing. It handles localized titles, includes a sidebar with organizer info and role-specific quick actions, and ensures consistent presentation across different user types.

tasks.name: Build Event Header Information Component
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive event header component that displays primary event information including title, subtitle, organizer details, and user-specific action buttons. This component serves as the main focal point at the top of the event details page with role-based functionality.
tasks.notes: Use Flowbite React components for buttons and dropdowns. Ensure proper handling of JSON translation fields from the database. Keep header clean and uncluttered. Focus on viewing experience with minimal role-specific additions.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Header component displays all event information correctly, handles multilingual content properly, shows appropriate role-based action buttons, renders responsively on all screen sizes, and maintains consistent layout across user types.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Event Header Information component has been successfully implemented as EventDetailsHeader.tsx. It displays event title, subtitle, organizer details with verification badge, and various event metadata (creation date, event type, status, registration fee, etc.). The component also includes role-specific content like statistics for event owners and properly handles multilingual content via useTranslations.

tasks.name: Develop Event Timeline and Deadlines Component
tasks.description: Implement a timeline component that visualizes important event dates including event start/end dates, abstract submission deadlines, full paper deadlines, and notification dates. Include countdown timers for upcoming deadlines and visual progress indicators.
tasks.notes: Use Flowbite React Timeline component for consistent styling. Implement proper date handling and formatting utilities. Consider adding real-time countdown updates with client-side JavaScript for active deadlines. Keep timeline purely informational for all user types.

relatedFiles.description: Timeline component for dates and deadlines

tasks.verificationCriteria: Timeline displays all event dates correctly, countdown timers show accurate remaining time, visual indicators properly reflect current submission phase, component handles past/future events appropriately, responsive layout works on all devices, and timeline appears identical for all user types.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Event Timeline and Deadlines Component has been successfully implemented in the file EventDetailsTimeline.tsx. The component effectively displays a chronological timeline of important event dates including event start/end, submission deadlines, review deadlines, and notification dates. It features countdown timers for upcoming deadlines, visually distinguishes between past and future events with appropriate styling, and formats dates according to the user's locale. The implementation uses Flowbite's recommended design patterns with custom styling, handles null dates appropriately, and provides a responsive layout. The component is identical for all user types as it's purely informational as required.

tasks.name: Create Event Location and Venue Component
tasks.description: Build a component that displays comprehensive location information including venue name, address, city, country, and provides integration points for future map functionality. Include travel and accommodation guidance section placeholder.
tasks.notes: Design component to support future Google Maps or OpenStreetMap integration. Use location icons from Flowbite or Heroicons. Consider adding structured data for SEO benefits. Focus on information display rather than user-specific functionality.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Location component displays all venue information clearly, handles missing location data gracefully, provides proper structure for future map integration, maintains consistent styling with other event detail components, and appears identical across user types.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Event Location and Venue Component has been successfully implemented as EventDetailsLocation.tsx. The component displays the event location information with a clean UI, provides actionable links for getting directions through Google Maps, and includes a function to copy the address to the clipboard. It properly handles cases where location information might be missing and integrates well with the rest of the event details page.

tasks.name: Implement Event Description and Topics Component
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive component for displaying event description, research topics, submission guidelines, and conference tracks. This component handles the main content area with proper text formatting and topic categorization.
tasks.notes: Use Flowbite React components for badges, cards, and expandable content. Ensure proper handling of HTML content in descriptions. Consider implementing rich text rendering for formatted content. Focus on content presentation rather than user-specific features.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Description component renders all text content properly, topics are displayed in organized manner, expandable sections work correctly, component handles multilingual content appropriately, text formatting enhances readability, and content appears identical across user types.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Event Description and Topics Component has been successfully implemented as EventDetailsDescription.tsx. The component presents comprehensive event information including problem statement, objectives, topics, submission guidelines, and organizer details. It properly handles multilingual content, displays topics with visually distinct badges, and organizes content into clean card sections with appropriate icons for each section. The component supports RTL layout for Arabic language and maintains consistent styling with other event components.

tasks.name: Build Role-Specific Action Component
tasks.description: Create a clean, role-specific action component that provides appropriate functionality based on user type: submission capabilities for researchers, management access for organizers, editing for admins. This component handles the main call-to-action area at the bottom of the event details page.
tasks.notes: Design component to seamlessly integrate with upcoming submission form functionality and management dashboard. Use consistent button styling and clear call-to-action patterns. Each user type should see one primary action that makes sense for their role.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Action component displays appropriate functionality for each user type, access control works correctly, subscription status affects researcher actions properly, component provides clear path to next steps, and UI remains clean and uncluttered.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Role-Specific Action Component has been successfully implemented as EventDetailsActions.tsx. The component provides differentiated functionality based on user role: event owners see event statistics, researchers can submit papers (conditional on deadlines), and general visitors have options to save/share the event. The implementation includes proper date handling for submission deadlines, subscription status checks, and a clean UI with appropriate actions for each user type.

tasks.name: Create Event Management Dashboard Page
tasks.description: Implement a separate management dashboard page at src/app/[locale]/events/[id]/manage/page.tsx for event owners to manage their events, view submissions, access analytics, and handle event-related tasks. This page is only accessible to the event owner organizer and admins.
tasks.notes: This page should only be accessible to the event owner organizer and admins. Implement proper access control and redirect unauthorized users. Use existing dashboard layout patterns from admin section for consistency.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Management page is only accessible to event owners and admins, provides comprehensive event management functionality, integrates with existing dashboard patterns, handles access control properly, and offers clear navigation back to event details.
tasks.analysisResult: Implementation of dynamic event details page and management dashboard for Eventy360 platform with comprehensive event information display, user-specific functionality, RTL internationalization support, and responsive design following existing architectural patterns. The implementation includes both the main event details page for viewing and a separate management dashboard for organizers.
tasks.summary: The Event Management Dashboard Page has been successfully implemented at src/app/[locale]/profile/events/[id]/manage/page.tsx. The page provides event owners with a comprehensive management interface including statistics (registrations, submissions, views), event status information, quick access to editing functionality, and tabs for managing different aspects of the event (overview, participants, submissions). The implementation includes proper authentication checks to ensure only event owners and admins can access the page, with automatic redirects for unauthorized users.
